node.content:
The PM Group is excited and incredibly proud to announce our new relationship with Triumph Over Kid Cancer, a non-profit organization born to fight and ultimately prevent cancer in pediatric patients.
 
Triumph Over Kid Cancer started as 13-year-old James A. Ragan’s dream. As a victim of cancer himself, James fought valiantly for seven years before ultimately succumbing to the illness. Although he’s no longer with us, his spirit, vision, and foundation live on. Triumph Over Kid Cancer has now grown into a cancer-fighting powerhouse, raising over $2 million since 2010.
 
While providing funding for cancer and research, Triumph Over Kid Cancer allows fellow members of the community to engage with the nonprofit by way of volunteering hours, attending special events, and donating directly to the cause.
 
“The beautiful thing about this company is that we help brands and brick and mortar businesses to tailor and send their message exactly where it needs to go,” said Bob Wills, PM Group founder, and CEO. “I love every day of work, but this business is the most rewarding when I get to help incredible causes like Triumph Over Kid Cancer. Many folks like to say that they care, but it’s exciting to actually realize that we’re making a difference here.” He added, “We’re really impacting lives.”
 
“We are so incredibly blessed and excited to be working with The PM Group” stated co-founder Mecklin Ragan. “We’ve seen what the agency has done around Texas. We couldn’t be more excited to have their advertising prowess, marketing expertise, and media influence in our corner,” she said.
 
The PM Group will be the media agency of record in San Antonio and Corpus Christi.
 
About The PM Group:
Marketing a non-profit’s unique products and services requires an often-unique set of skills. At The PM Group, we possess the knowledge, the skill sets and above all a proven track record of helping non-profits achieve their goals. For years we have proudly represented many of the city’s leading non-profit organizations by raising millions of dollars on their behalf. Specifically, we focus on agency philanthropy, at risk children, education, first responders, family services, medical research, pet rescue, and veterans.

node.content:
A simple service promise has quickly become a drastic service commitment. We’re proud to announce that Nissan of Boerne has been officially recognized with its second consecutive Nissan Award of Excellence from Nissan of America in 2017 for outstanding customer service.
 
The second consecutive win in two years marks Nissan of Boerne a back to back winner of the award, as well as one of the most renowned dealerships in the area. With so much competition at the corporate, regional, and local levels, obtaining the award of excellence from Nissan is no small feat.
 
One simply can’t afford to run a dealership with minimal selection. Nissan of Boerne kept this in mind when establishing the biggest Nissan dealership by square footage in the entire state! Nissan of Boerne isn’t all lot and no talk. They have operated from day one as customer-centric. Their profound approach of buying in bulk and trying to move numbers instead of larger margins on individual cars has helped them to become one of the top-selling dealerships in the area. With prices and service, you are comfortable with, Nissan of Boerne is trying to be different.
 
Their excellence doesn’t stop once you drive off in your new car. Boasting one of the biggest service bays in the area, they pride themselves in ample availability. In the rare event that one of their reliable Nissan cars needs service, it can be done quickly and professionally with all certified Nissan parts. For the above reasons and more, Nissan of Boerne is surely a dealership to keep in mind for those looking for Nissans near south central Texas, Austin, Boerne, and San Antonio.
 
At the PM Group, we realize that behind every great salesperson is a strategic marketing plan. In today’s highly competitive world, “good” only produces “decent” rewards. “Great” sometimes garners “good” compensation. To do “great” in 2017, you truly have to be “excellent”. Nissan of Boerne’s second consecutive recognition of their excellence speaks volumes to their commitment.
 
We could not be prouder to work with Nissan of Boerne and are overjoyed with their recent success both in terms of both sales and more importantly, customer service.
 
Check out Nissan of Boerne’s website..
 
– The PM Group
 
About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. Over the past 30 years, the agency has continued to grow and is currently listed as San Antonio’s Largest Advertising Agency by the San Antonio Business Journal. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profit, real estate and fitness. In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
The new CSN television network has added more UTSA broadcasts to its inaugural college football lineup.
 

 
Quarter Moon Productions‘ new Citywide Sports Network won’t broadcast its first live event until Sept. 16, but it’s already adding content and securing new affiliate partners across Texas.
 
CSN, which will kick off its live sports programming with the University of Texas at San Antonio’s Sept. 16 home football game against Southern, is adding two more Roadrunners telecasts. As a result, the new network will now televise all but three of UTSA’s 11 games this season.
 
CSN has locked up agreements to televise UTSA road games against Texas State and North Texas on Sept. 23 and Oct. 14, respectively. Those contests will air locally on KMYS-TV (CW 35) and KCWX-TV.
 
As CSN picks up programming, it’s also expanding its geographic reach and adding network affiliates in Austin, Dallas and Houston. UTSA’s game against Texas State will also be broadcast in Austin on KEYE-TV via CSN.
 
Meanwhile, Tribune Broadcasting’s Dallas affiliate, KDAF-TV, will carry CSN’s telecast of the Roadrunners’ Conference USA match up against North Texas.
 
CEO of Quarter Moon Productions Bob Wills said the new network’s previously scheduled telecast of UTSA’s Oct. 21 home game against Rice, set to air on CW 35 in San Antonio, will now also be picked up by Tribune-owned stations in Houston and Dallas.
 
“We are building out the CSN affiliate network,” Wills said. “Next year, we hope to have stations all over the state. But you’ve got to crawl, walk and run. We are finding that stations like the content.”
 
One of the lures CSN has used to hook affiliates is the fact that all of its football telecasts, with the exception of Texas State’s Nov. 11 home game against Georgia State, will be broadcast in prime time.
 
CSN also plans to televise 14 NCAA men’s basketball games involving UTSA, University of the Incarnate Word, St. Mary’s University, Trinity University and Texas State. All of those games will tip off in prime time.
 
CSN will need advertiser support to be successful long term. Network officials said the early signs are positive.
 
“Getting ourselves launched as a new enterprise has its challenges and opportunities. But we’ve got all these games cleared and sponsors continue to come on board,” Wills said.
 
CSN officials expect to break even financially through this first broadcast season, which runs through March with the basketball schedule.
 
“We are rolling,” Wills said. “We are in multiple markets and now we just need to start producing games.
 
Scott Bailey
Reporter/Project Coordinator
San Antonio Business Journal
 
About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. Over the past 30 years, the agency has continued to grow and is currently listed as San Antonio’s Largest Advertising Agency by the San Antonio Business Journal. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profit, real estate and fitness. In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
In honor of National Read a Book Day, we are sharing some of The PM Group’s favorite books.
 

 

If you like to escape into a world of fiction and fantasy, we recommend:

Lightning Thief – Rick Riordan
Harry Potter – JK Rowling
Ender’s Game – Orson Scott Card
War of the Worlds – H. G. Wells
 The Hobbit – J.R.R. Tolkien
To the Far Blue Mountains – Louis L’Amour
 

If you want something more down to earth and pertinent to everyday life, try these non-fiction and self-help titles:

Don’t Sweat the Small Stuff – Richard Carlson
The Last Lecture – Jeffrey Zaslow
Rich Dad, Poor Dad – Robert Kiyosaki and Sharon Lechter
The Richest Man in Babylon – George Samuel Clason
The Millionaire Next Door – Thomas J. Stanley
 

If you are hungry for some more marketing wisdom and advertising prowess, check out:

Confessions of an Advertising Man – David Ogilvy
The Tipping Point – Malcolm Gladwell
Buyology: Truth and Lies About What We Buy – Martin Lindstrom
The Copywriter’s Handbook – Robert W. Bly
 
Whatever you’re in the mood for, we have plenty of page-turners to choose from. Happy reading!
 
– The PM Group
 
About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. Over the past 30 years, the agency has continued to grow and is currently listed as San Antonio’s Largest Advertising Agency by the San Antonio Business Journal. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, nonprofit, real estate and fitness. In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area nonprofit organizations in the last 15 years! It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
Many of the major players in nearly every industry have one thing in common. They use a thoughtful and engaging blog on their professional website to create website traffic.
 
Hosting a company blog drives traffic by creating an increasingly large pool of searchable content. Blogs are essentially the latest tool in the pocket of companies who are working to find yet another way to provide value, increase their credibility, and reach the elusive potential customer or client.
 

Searchable Content

Modern digital marketers have a common phrase they often fall back on: “Content is King”. Not only does well- written content engage and inform your audience, but it also creates hidden pathways back to your company website online. By including industry-relevant buzzwords or “keywords” in your content, you join the conversation around your business and share your perspective with the relevant readers.
 
If you write blog content, you are growing your business by giving someone else free information. Go figure! It’s basically the digital marketing equivalent of giving samples at a grocery store. They know that if the consumer likes what they try, they’ll always come back for more.
 

Establishing Industry Credibility

Once you’ve established a deep and thorough foundation of industry related content, you can gradually begin to build credibility in your field as more readers come across your content and find the information to be valuable. Continually churning out valuable content transforms your website from merely a vendor into an industry-wide resource for whatever you sell or specialize in.
 
This proven expertise, in turn, has the potential to convert audience members into leads and leads into customers thus growing your business. Customers always like to work with the best in the business. For example, you wouldn’t go to an unproven surgeon for a life-threatening operation. By delighting your customers and creating leads that are loyal to your company, you even create the potential for proud promoters of your brand and services.
 

The freedom of creativity, while still getting down to business.

Last but certainly not least, an additional benefit to having a company blog on your website is it creates an entirely new channel for expression and personality. From creating original graphics to writing outside the box, you are free from the typical restrictions imposed upon by social mediums. That’s not to say that you shouldn’t always promote your blogs on social media, but the content on a blog tied to your website is protected from being reduced, watered-down, or limited by social media platforms.
 
As long as the posts are well written, contain credible and relevant information, and are packed with keywords you are ready. Ready to drive traffic, increase company credibility and ultimately grow your business.
 
Till next time!
 
-The PM Group

The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, nonprofit, real estate and fitness. In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
It’s been a very busy few weeks for SA YES and the momentum isn’t slowing down.

Last week SA YES concluded their first ever Drive Away 4 Education Truck Raffle sponsored by Ancira Auto Group and Sinclair Broadcast Group.  With over $113,000 raised in raffle ticket purchases, SA YES will be able to help 22,600 children in the San Antonio and surrounding areas.
 
And what better time to see the results of SA YES’ efforts than during their annual school supply distribution as the children of San Antonio and Corpus Christi start a new school year.  In the last three weeks of August, SA YES will be visiting 48 schools within 5 school districts and distributing over 26,000 school supply kits by hand.
 
Some of The PM Group staff have joined them by volunteering their time to pass out school supplies and even dress up in costumes to get the students excited for a new year of learning.
 
If you want to find out more about SA YES’ incredible mission make sure to visit their website and learn more.
 
About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. Over the past 30 years, the agency has continued to grow and is currently listed as San Antonio’s Largest Advertising Agency by the San Antonio Business Journal. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profit, real estate and fitness. In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
The PM Group President, Fran Yanity, along with American Heart Association representative, Nora Silva, sat down with SA Living and Daytime at Nine this week to continue the conversation about the importance of heart health for women and the Red Sofa Tour sponsored by The PM Group.

It’s a staggering statistic that 1 in 3 women are killed from heart disease and stroke in the U.S., but by encouraging the conversation and education about heart health, the American Heart Association’s Go Red for Women movement and Fran Yanity hope to lower that number.

As the Red Sofa Tour makes its way throughout San Antonio until May of 2018, the Go Red for Women movement invites women to sit down, talk about ways to improve their health, and have fun by sharing the message on social media.

Find out more about what you could do to lead a healthier lifestyle by checking out the segments below.

News4SA – Go Red for Women

Fox San Antonio – How You Can Become Heart Healthy

To find out their next stop on the tour and more visit the Red Sofa Tour website.

In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
Our industry is full of creatives waiting to share their craft and National Joke Day is a fun outlet to showcase their talents in a way we don’t normally get to see.  We’ve picked our favorite marketing joke thanks to Hubspot.

Here are the jokes that didn’t make the cut.  Share with us your favorites on our Facebook page.

How many marketers does it take to screw in a light bulb?

via GIPHY

None – they’ve automated it

What does the new Chips Ahoy marketing director do her first day on the job?

via GIPHY

Enable cookies.

What’s a personality trait of a bad marketer?

via GIPHY

Anti-social.

Why did the marketer get off the trampoline?

via GIPHY

He was worried about his bounce rate.

Why do marketers make such good wide receivers?

via GIPHY

Because they always stay inbounds.

node.content:
Almost half of U.S. millennial grocery shoppers polled this year said they have purchased some groceries in an online-only store, which is dramatic change from just two years ago….and they are not alone!


The findings on millennial grocery shopping habits mirror a trend of consumers across all age groups increasingly getting their groceries among internet pure plays, although at a more modest level.
A February 2017 survey by food industry research firm Food Marketing Institute (FMI) found that 43% of millennial respondents shopped for groceries at online-only retailers at least occasionally, a rate almost 80% higher than in 2015.
Consumers ages 39 to 52 shopped for groceries at online-only retailers at about half the rate of millennials, though the proportion who did so was up just 2 percentage points from 2016 and 7 percentage points the year prior.
Not surprisingly, 53- to 71-year-olds and seniors ages 72 and older were less likely to grocery shop from online-only retailers. In addition, there was a drop-off: The percentages of respondents in these age cohorts who said they bought groceries digitally were lower in this year’s study than in 2016.
While multiple generations have taken to buying groceries online, shopping with online-only retailers for these items at such a high rate is unique to millennials. Moreover, the survey found that the number of millennials buying groceries almost exclusively from online-only retailers has risen significantly.
However, there is a narrow range of grocery products that millennials are likely to buy from pure-play ecommerce purveyors—items like baby food, cleaning supplies and pet food, according to FMI. Millennial respondents in the survey tended to avoid using ecommerce for fresh food items like meat, seafood, dairy and produce.
These findings are similar to another study that found many US internet users apprehensive about purchasing perishables online.  Younger people polled in June 2017 by Morning Consult were more likely to have groceries delivered, but food quality was the most important consideration when shopping digitally.
Millennial adoption of online-only grocery shopping points to an increasingly digital future for that activity. For example, the purchase of Whole Foods Market by Amazon in June sent a signal that the ecommerce giant, for one, intends to take millennials’ grocery shopping preferences seriously.
Article courtesy of:  eMarketer Retail

In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
The PM Group celebrated with the community at the new Walk-On’s Bistreaux & Bar VIP event earlier this month. The UTSA Marching Band played for the attendees and Ron Ellis, UTSA Marching Band Director, received a $4,000 donation for their participation. Their performance enhanced the game-day atmosphere that San Antonio’s newest sports bar provides.  Director of Athletics, Lynn Hickey, and UTSA Head Football Coach, Frank Wilson, also joined in the festivities.

node.content:
Walk-Ons Bistreaux & Bar celebrated their San Antonio grand opening the weekend of July 15th.  The restaurant was overflowing with excited VIP party guests, news groups, and restaurant staff and executives.
 
See what local news station, News 4 San Antonio, had to say about new New Orleans Saints quarterback Drew Brees new connection to San Antonio.
 
About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. Over the past 30 years, the agency has continued to grow and is currently listed as San Antonio’s Largest Advertising Agency by the San Antonio Business Journal. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profit, real estate and fitness. In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years!  It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
We’re proud to share the news that Fran Yanity, The PM Group’s President and the 2018 chair for the American Heart Association’s Go Red for Women movement, hosted her first Executive Leadership Team meeting today.  She and this dynamic team are already hitting the ground running in securing partners for the annual luncheon in February 2018 and we look forward to sharing the results of their hard work in the coming months.
 
Did you know?
 
Because of the American Heart Association and its Go Red for Women movement, more than 627,000 women have been saved from heart disease, and 285 fewer women are dying each day.
 
Women’s health guidelines, created by the American Heart Association, have educated millions of healthcare providers to recognize and treat heart disease in women.
 
Possibly the most important statistic is that the number of women aware that heart disease is the no. 1 killer and stroke is the no. 5 killer of women and men in the U.S. has jumped from 22 percent to well over 50 percent.
 
To learn more about how you can support the Go Red for Women movement, contact Fran Yanity at fran.yanity@thepmgrp.com. For information about women and heart health, visit www.goredforwomen.org.
 
About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. Over the past 30 years, the agency has continued to grow and is currently listed as San Antonio’s Largest Advertising Agency by the San Antonio Business Journal. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profit, real estate and fitness. In addition, the agency hosts an annual Charity Gala every February that to date has raised over $2.5 million for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years!  It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.
